name: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY)
description: World's largest health insurance scheme providing secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
benefits: Covers 3 days pre-hospitalization and 15 days post-hospitalization
Covers all pre-existing conditions from Day 1

name: PM-JAY Senior Citizens (70+)
description: Universal health coverage for all senior citizens aged 70 years and above.
benefits: Exclusive ₹5 Lakh top-up if already in PM-JAY
Standalone ₹5 Lakh cover for non-PM-JAY families

name: Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN)
description: Financial assistance to patients living below poverty line for treatment of life-threatening diseases.
benefits: One-time grant for treatment in super-specialty government hospitals

name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
description: Safe motherhood intervention to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality.

eligibility.location: Public or accredited private health facilities

name: Arogyasri (Dr. YSR Aarogyasri)
description: Flagship health scheme of AP providing universal health coverage.

name: Swasthya Sathi
description: Smart card based cashless health insurance for all residents of West Bengal.

name: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana (BSKY)
description: Universal health coverage for families in Odisha.
benefits: Free healthcare in all State Govt facilities
Cashless treatment in 800+ empanelled private hospitals
Special higher coverage for women family members

name: Mukhyamantri Amrutam (MA Yojana)
description: Tertiary care health scheme for BPL and lower middle-class families in Gujarat.
benefits: Covers Heart, Kidney, Cancer, Brain injuries

name: Karunya Health Care Scheme (KASP)
description: Comprehensive health insurance scheme of Kerala integrated with PM-JAY.
benefits: Coverage in both public and empanelled private hospitals
Free medical services in government hospitals

name: Deen Dayal Swasthya Seva Yojana (DDSSY)
description: Universal health insurance for the people of Goa.
benefits: Annual coverage for medicines and diagnostics

name: Ayushman Uttarakhand (AB-UP)
description: Universal health coverage for all residents of Uttarakhand.
benefits: Integrated with PM-JAY for national portability

name: Himcare Scheme
description: Health insurance for families not covered under PM-JAY in Himachal Pradesh.

name: Rajiv Gandhi Jeevandayee Aarogya Yojana
description: Legacy health insurance scheme for poor families in Maharashtra, now part of MJPJAY.

name: Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS)
description: State-funded universal health insurance scheme for Nagaland.
benefits: Cashless treatment for secondary and tertiary care
Portability across India in PM-JAY network

name: Aushman Bharat – Sarbat Sehat Bima Yojana
description: Integrated health insurance scheme for families in Punjab.

name: Rajasthan Mukhya Mantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana
description: Massive universal health coverage scheme of Rajasthan.
benefits: Covers liver, heart, and bone marrow transplants

name: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram (JSSK)
description: Entitlement to free and cashless services to pregnant women and sick newborns.

eligibility.category: All pregnant women and infants (up to 1 year)

benefits: Free drugs, diagnostics, and diet during stay
Free transport from home to facility and back

name: Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK)
description: Early identification and intervention for children from birth to 18 years.
benefits: Screening for 4 Ds (Defects, Deficiencies, Diseases, Developmental delays)
Free surgical interventions (e.g. Cleft lip, Congenital heart disease)
Management via District Early Intervention Centres (DEIC)
